Defendants Sbot Technologies, Llc And Maplebear Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. HASSAN ABURISH, Esq. of SV Employment Law Firm PC (Tel: 650-265-0222) for Plaintiff.
Atty. ADAM CASHMAN, Esq. of Singer Cashman LLP (Tel: 415-500-6080) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SBOT TECHNOLOGIES, INC., MAPLEBEAR, INC. D/B/A INSTACART Defendants Sbot Technologies, Llc And Maplebear Inc.'S Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed motion to seal is granted.

California Rule of Court 2.550(d) provides that the Court may order a record sealed if the following five facts are established: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest."

Here, the third-party privacy rights in the text messages override the right of public access, a substantial probability exists that the overriding right will be prejudiced without sealing, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist. In addition, defendants' interest in sealing its confidential financial information (especially information subject to contractual non-disclosure obligations) overrides the public's right of access. (See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Ct. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1281.) There exists a substantial probability that defendants' interest will be prejudiced without sealing, the proposed order is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Jaime Ply, CSR# 13323 (Tel: 714-316-3757/ E-mail: jplycsr@aol.com).(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Seal Portions Of Documents And Information Filed In Connection To Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Proceedings And Transfer To Supreme Court For New York County - Commercial Division
Off Calendar Feb-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JOHN SCHMIT's Notice Of Motion To Seal Portions Of Documents And Information Filed In Connection To Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Proceedings And Transfer To Supreme Court For New York County - Commercial Division.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion to seal portions of documents and information filed in connection to defendants' motion to dismiss or, in the alternative, stay proceedings and transfer to Supreme Court for New York County - commercial division" is denied without prejudice.

Should plaintiff wish to pursue his sealing requests, he shall do the following: First, provide to the court and opposing counsel in one clearly indexed package all "portions of documents and information" he wants withheld from the public court record. Second, in tabular form, enumerate each specific fact he seeks to have withheld, citing to its location in the clearly indexed package. Third, in the same table, enumerate next to each specific fact the specific reason(s) for withholding it. (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894; CRC 2.550.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Proceedings And Transfer To Supreme Court For New York County - Commercial Division
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. HASSAN A. ABURISH, Esq. of SV Employment Law Firm PC (Tel: 650-265-0222) for plaintiff;
Atty. DOUG TILLEY, Esq. of Singer Cashman LLP (Tel: 415-500-6080) for defendants SBot Technologies LLC and Maplebear Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SBOT TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, and MAPLEBEAR, INC. D/B/A INSTACART's Motion To Seal Portions Of Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Proceedings And Transfer To Supreme Court For New York County - Commercial Division.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to seal is denied without prejudice. To enable a court to make the specific findings required by California Rule of Court 2.550(d), a party moving to seal court records must present "specific enumeration of the facts sought to be withheld and specific reasons for withholding them." (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) Here, the sealing motion papers merely report that certain documents have been labeled "confidential" under the discovery protective order in the case. However, the protective order explicitly provides that it "creates no right to file Protected Material under seal without complying with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 et seq." (Id. at 10:23-24.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANTS SBOT TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, MAPLEBEAR, INC. D/B/A INSTACART Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendants Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross-Complaint

Defendants' sealing motion is denied without prejudice. A party's designation of material as "confidential" standing alone does not meet the requirements of California Rule of Court 2.550(d). Defendants' motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part. The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend the request for penalties. The motion to strike the request for punitive damages is denied.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Plaintiff to file a Second Amended Complaint by October 28, 2022.

The prevailing party is to submit a revised proposed order reflecting the Court's modifications.
